title: Analytical procedure for studying land empathy in Participatory Scenario Planning
author: Petrescu-Mag, Ruxandra Malina
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This document provides a detailed account of the analytical procedure used in the study "Collective care and rural transitions: The role of empathy in addressing land degradation" (submitted to  Sociologia Ruralis ). It supplements the methodology section of the main manuscript by offering a step-by-step description of the Interpretive Discourse Analysis (IDA) framework applied to examine how empathy emerged and operated within a Participatory Scenario Planning (PSP) workshop conducted in the Târnava Mare microregion, Transylvania, Romania. 
 This material is intended for researchers interested in the methodological details of discourse-based approaches to studying empathy in participatory environmental governance, rural sustainability transitions, and post-communist land stewardship contexts.
